what_it_does: Builds a fixed 1600x900 presentation screen with two mobile app mockups for a work sheet and ledger, plus a right-side interpretive content column describing density, gold action, seasonal order, and nested plates.
observations: The document is a self-contained HTML artboard with inline CSS and inline JavaScript.
The left zone contains two phone mockups labeled as sheet and ledger views.
The first phone shows a bench/work screen with a stat band, two nested plates, and a bottom primary action.
The second phone shows ledger filters for seasons and seven generated ledger rows.
The right column contains a superhead, headline, four fragment statements, a generated consequences box, a circular V mark, and a hatched footer wash.
Runtime content is populated through DNA.org.icon and DNA.org.consequencesBox from /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
The visual language uses warm page colors, gold accents, hatch patterns, nested surfaces, mono labels, and a dark translucent bottom rail.
standouts: The file makes the contrast between one prominent action and controlled ledger density explicit in both layout and copy.
Two adjacent phone mockups show related app states rather than a single isolated screen.
Hatched gold bands and nested plates are repeated as structural motifs.
Season filters, ledger rows, stats, and bottom navigation are all represented as concrete interface elements.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties from /surface/styles/vars.css for color, type, spacing, and radius tokens.
Keeps the composition as a fixed-size artboard centered in the viewport.
Uses Plus Jakarta Sans for main UI text and IBM Plex Mono for labels, times, and compact metadata.
Uses inline JavaScript to inject repeated rows, icons, navigation rails, and the consequences box.
Uses class-based styling with short semantic class names and a few inline border-color styles for seasonal pills.
concerns: The layout is fixed at 1600x900 and is not responsive within this file.
The file depends on /surface/styles/vars.css and /surface/runtime/organisms.js being present at absolute paths.
Google Fonts are loaded remotely, so rendering depends on network availability.
Repeated rail icon generation is duplicated for the two phones.
The row HTML is generated by template interpolation, though the data is local constants in this file.
notes: A presentation artboard showing a two-screen app substrate where a primary work action and a denser ledger view share one visual system.
questions: Is this intended only as a static presentation piece, or should the phone mockups function as an interactive app surface?
What exact contract does /surface/runtime/organisms.js provide for icon names and consequencesBox output?
Should the seasonal filter pills imply active filtering behavior, or are they purely visual in this version?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents app working screens for a sheet and ledger, with the visual hierarchy and explanatory copy centered on gold action, density, and ordered ledger structure.
intention_signals: It seems intended to define an app-screen substrate where a calm ledger system and a focused daily work action can coexist through shared tokens, seasonal structure, nested panels, and restrained gold emphasis.
novelty: The file frames mobile app UI as a material ledger system, using terms like bench, seams, grain, cut, keeping, and seasons as interface structure rather than ordinary app labels.
contribution: It contributes a concrete app-screen pattern with paired sheet and ledger states, a thumb-reach primary action, dense but orderly rows, seasonal filter pills, nested plate/inset hierarchy, stat bands over hatch texture, and dark glass bottom navigation.
summary_for_embedding: A fixed HTML/CSS/JS presentation artboard showing two mobile working-screen mockups, one for a daily bench sheet with a gold action and one for a seasonal ledger with dense rows, alongside interpretive copy and generated consequence metrics.
intention_for_embedding: This was for expressing a ledger-oriented app design substrate that balances focused action, controlled density, seasonal ordering, and nested visual hierarchy.
